text.content:
This service is unlocked once Supply Lines has been completed. It takes place on Sublevel 2 of the Afterlife, and is maintained by Mr. Connors (weapons & armor), Miss Piercing (Cyberware, Electronics, and Crafting Supplies) and Madame Garcia (food, drugs, home supplies, vehicles, and clothing).

Cost

The Afterlife's Eagles are suddenly worth significantly more than the cash they could be redeemed for - in case that wasn't already clear.

  • All of the Afterlife's booths are stocked with any item in-category worth 100eb or less. 1 Eagle = 200eb in credit towards these items.

  • Otherwise, once items are rolled, they use the following prices at GM discretion, depending on demand:

    • 500eb = 2-4 Eagles

    • 1000eb = 6-9 Eagles

    • Luxury = 10+ Eagles

    • Super Luxury = 20+ Eagles

  • For obvious reasons, these prices vary depending on demand and item versatility - for vehicles in particular, an air vehicle at a certain price may be, in practice, worth more Eagles than a groundcar of equivalent book value. Likewise for particular highly in-demand weapons, pieces of cyberware, etc.

Function

Every week on Sunday, the Afterlife's 3 market fixers reserve a niche of their garage to host a Night Market with the above prices. The event is quite a happening, but free to attend for anyone who pays to enter the establishment (note: being comped to pursue mercwork doesn't count - pay to play, baby!)

The market itself contains a number of valuable items which depend on your party's size.

Each player in your party may choose to roll twice on any combination of tables (note: improved tables have been included in NC G&C - see the rollable table compendium for details).

If a player chooses, they can spend one of their rolls buying clothing, instead. If they do so, they pay a price in Eagles for the relevant clothing items (note - items worth 100eb or less are still 2-for-1). Once they've described their new outfit, they gain a +1 personal rep bonus for the next in-game week, starting the first time they wear their new outfit in public. This bonus does not stack.
